posts.thread_title: I Stay Away...From nnEMF
posts.author: Anthony Smith
posts.content: Walk around the outside and see where it is coming from. Normally one side is the strongest, then look for towers. If no towers on that side close by good chance it is the neighbors WiFi. In single story buildings shielding the fence with wire mesh (metal window screen) can be a quick easy fix.

posts.thread_title: THE ANCIENTS...........WHAT DID THEY KNOW?
posts.author: Jack Kruse
posts.content: The Ocean is already seeping beneath the land of Yucatán, and this phenomenon is growing stronger by the day. Though we often see the peninsula as jungle, beaches, and crystal-clear cenotes, something astonishing is happening beneath the surface: ocean water slowly infiltrates through the soil and travels through massive underground systems. Yucatán is primarily formed of limestone, a highly porous type of rock that, over thousands of years, has allowed the creation of caves, underground rivers, and interconnected cenotes. That's why there are enormous flooded caverns and hidden currents that course through the subsurface practically from the Caribbean all the way to various points across the peninsula. Many scientists continue to study these systems because they are considered among the largest and most important on the planet. And the most surprising part is that there are still areas that remain completely unexplored. It's no coincidence that for the Maya, cenotes were sacred places. Beyond being sources of water, they were seen as gateways to the underworld and spaces connected to the forces of nature. While cities, roads, and jungle exist above... below, a gigantic hidden water system continues to move silently beneath our feet. And perhaps that's what makes Yucatán so fascinating: a land where the sea and the earth remain connected in a way we're still trying to understand.

posts.thread_title: I ain't dead yet mf'er
posts.author: Geoff Robinson
posts.content: For the first 3+ years I went into the sausage grinder that is "pain management". From 2013-2016 I walked blindly, accepting that the centralized medical system was the only option. This gave me a thorough experience with the medical-industrial complex, that I trusted for so long and still the mass majority of the public does as well. I had reached a brick wall with pain meds not working, dosages increasing, side effects becoming more common and eventually cold turkey'd it at the end of 2016. It was still some months, and torturous experiences before starting the journey into being responsible for my own health and finding Jack's podcasts. Embracing the change and not having much to lose I managed to live a pretty decent life from 2017-2020. The reality was I was able to live and do things that I haven't heard anyone with my condition doing. It's hard for the programming and conditioning to hang on when you don't just see the truth or understand the truth but you feel the truth. My condition worsened and the greater environment around me got worse. In 2021 I had to go back on pain meds to manage day to day living. After listening to a bunch of Q&A's this last week I'm having new concerns over the impact of my meds. I cut it down to just a pain med and non-benzo muscle relaxer.....but hearing the benzo/Jordan Peterson/GABA story I know now it's destroying me all the same. Along with that I'm wondering how bad of D+ bombs they are..... Plan is to get off all meds permanently AS…

posts.thread_title: WHY YOU MUST BE DISCERNING WITH AI
posts.author: Johan Lindøm
posts.content: AI is just a tool. Physically it’s nothing more than software running on silicon chips and electricity — exactly like any other computer program. You can control every single input you give Grok. Soon I’ll probably run my own local model on my own hardware with only the data I choose — the exact opposite of centralized cloud systems. Rejecting a tool before you’ve even tested it isn’t skepticism. It’s fear of change. I use it as a tool. You should try it some day.
